title: The Deep Eastern Convergence

features.name: the-oxidized-post
features.desc-on-touch: A fence post stands in both the paddock and the digital overlay—you can see the rust colour shifting to RGB values and back. The boundary it marks has become a coordinate.

features.name: the-surveyor-s-cottage
features.desc-on-touch: A small house rendered in ANSI, but its walls are overlaid with graph paper—maps visible through the cottage walls, cadastral diagrams fading in and out of the brickwork.

features.name: the-circuit-board-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: A trace of copper-coloured lines marks the eastern edge where eucalyptus and circuit converge—patterned like both a fence line and a semiconductor.

features.name: the-version-conflict-marker
features.desc-on-touch: Three identical posts stand in slightly different colours—v1, v2, v3 of the same boundary, each one canonical in a different version of the property records.

inscription: does it matter that the fence and the circuit mark the same line if they can't agree on what either one was first

file.name: Eastern_Circuit_Boundary_Survey_v1_v2_v3_Conflicted_Merge.shp
file.note: ERROR: metadata indicates three simultaneous canonical origins; all three are correct but mutually exclusive

exits.label: back toward the circuit's original oxidation
further east where the code fully overrides the paddock
toward the archive's own property records, where convergence started
